final_prompt.prompt:
Create an editorial still-life photo embodying wabi-sabi aesthetics, featuring a rough stoneware vase with bare dried branches on a weathered wooden table.

Style/medium: photorealistic 35mm film still-life photography, wabi-sabi aesthetic in the spirit of Japanese minimalist photography — imperfection, impermanence, and incompleteness as visual language
Composition: low oblique angle, natural window light entering from the upper-left, shallow depth of field, unhurried negative space around the subject
Subject: handmade rough stoneware vase with uneven glaze, asymmetric silhouette, holding sparse bare dried branches with delicate twigs; surface shows kiln marks and minor chips
Scene/backdrop: aged wooden table with peeling paint, visible grain, stains, and worn patches; plain muted wall in soft shadow behind
Lighting/mood: soft raking natural daylight from a side window, warm and cool simultaneously, casting long gentle shadows that reveal surface texture; contemplative and quietly melancholic
Color palette: desaturated earth tones — ash white, clay grey, weathered wood brown, and pale straw — unified by low saturation and subtle tonal contrast
Materials/textures: coarse stoneware with matte uneven glaze, dry cracked wood grain, papery bark of bare branches, dust-soft shadow edges
Avoid: artificial studio lighting, over-saturated colors, symmetrical composition, pristine surfaces, plastic-looking glaze, decorative flowers, busy background
